pi_items.source: GitHub release https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023) and installation script verification
OpenBCI documentation confirms SYNTHETIC (algorithmic) data generation mode is supported: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/

pi_items.metadata_value: Active synthetic EEG session with Time Series widget visible
pi_items.verified_value: Active synthetic EEG session with Time Series widget visible
pi_items.source: OpenBCI GUI widget documentation confirms Time Series widget exists: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/ - Time Series is described as 'the main widget for displaying biosensing data' and 'processes and displays electrophysiological signals in real time'

Task comparison analysis: start_synthetic_session is marked 'easy', enable_expert_mode is 'easy-medium', add_fft_widget is 'easy-medium', and set_bandpass_filter is 'medium'. The start_synthetic_session task only requires selecting a data source from a list and clicking START SESSION, making it the simplest task in the suite.

privileged_info_summary: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 is a verified real release (August 21, 2023) for EEG data visualization and analysis on Linux. It supports SYNTHETIC mode for algorithmic EEG data generation with 8-channel simulated signals. The Time Series widget is the primary visualization component for real-time signal display. The task involves selecting the SYNTHETIC data source and starting a session, classified as 'easy' difficulty based on comparison with other tasks in the same suite.
